questions.text: What is the primary purpose of Opik tracing?
options: Capture hierarchical LLM calls, tool steps, and agent activity for debugging and analysis
Replace Git version control for prompt files
Host production LLM inference at scale
Manage Kubernetes clusters for AI workloads
questions.explanation: Opik tracing records the full chain of events in a generative AI request — LLM calls, retrievals, tools, and agent steps — as spans you can inspect in the UI.

options: A human reviewer must approve every trace manually
An LLM scores outputs against criteria like relevance or hallucination
The largest model in a cluster always wins A/B tests
Opik replaces judges in legal document review
questions.explanation: LLM-as-a-judge uses a model to automatically evaluate outputs — for example checking answer relevance, detecting hallucinations, or running moderation — at experiment or production scale.

questions.text: How does this onboarding tool run Opik locally?
options: It downloads a proprietary Comet-only binary with no source code
It clones comet-ml/opik and starts the stack with opik.sh (Docker)
It requires a paid Comet Cloud account before any local UI loads
It embeds Opik inside the onboarding-ui Vite bundle only
questions.explanation: deploy-locally.sh clones the open-source Opik repo to OPIK_PATH (default ../opik) and runs ./opik.sh, which brings up the self-hosted frontend and API on port 5173.

questions.text: What happens when you send a message in the chat demo during onboarding?
options: Nothing is logged; the demo is UI-only
Messages go to Ollama for inference and traces are sent to Opik via the SDK
Messages are posted publicly to GitHub Issues
Only browser localStorage stores the conversation
questions.explanation: The chat demo calls Ollama (llama3.1:latest) for responses and instruments the flow with the Opik SDK so verify-opik-wiring.sh can confirm traces land in your local Opik project.

questions.text: What branch naming pattern does this tool use for Opik contributions?
options: main-only commits with no branches
feature/random-uuid
opik-onboarding-tool-97115104-contribution-{N}
dependabot/npm_and_yarn
questions.explanation: create-contribution-branch.sh creates branches on the Opik repo (not this tool repo) matching opik-onboarding-tool-97115104-contribution-{N}, incrementing N until a free name is found.
